title: Provenance sidecar + recoverable, informative overwrite
description: Add a shared writeManifestWithProvenance helper (src/fleet/manifest-write.ts) and route both fleet expand and fleet expand-deep Step-4 writes through it: emit a provenance sidecar, preserve the prior manifest as .bak on overwrite, and print an informative non-blocking notice. ADR-4 single shared default path is preserved; provenance lives only in the sidecar.
estimatedFiles: src/fleet/manifest-write.ts (new — writeManifestWithProvenance, ManifestProvenance type, relative-age formatter, injectable clock)
src/fleet/index.ts (edit — replace the two Step-4 write blocks at :207-224 and :343-360 with helper calls)
src/fleet/expand-deep-critique.test.ts (edit — update overwrite-notice assertion if present)

successCriteria.description: npm run build (tsc) is clean after adding manifest-write.ts and wiring index.ts.
Strict typecheck passes; no explicit any; manifest-write.ts imports only types from providers/manifest as needed via import type; ESM .js extensions; provider-agnostic (no SDK imports).
ESLint clean on the new + changed files.

AC1 — on a successful write the helper creates `${outPath}.meta.json` with { command, goal, critique, childCount, timestamp }: for fleet expand command==='fleet expand'/critique===false; for fleet expand-deep command==='fleet expand-deep'/critique reflects --critique; goal is the RAW goal; childCount===manifest.children.length. Asserted by reading the sidecar in a test.

AC2+AC3 — first write (no pre-existing manifest) writes manifest+sidecar, prints NO replace notice, creates NO .bak. Overwrite moves the PRIOR manifest bytes to `${outPath}.bak` BEFORE writing the new manifest (reading .bak returns the previous content), and writes the new manifest atomically (tmp+rename).

AC4+AC5 — overwrite notice (with a fixed injected clock) deterministically reports prior command, goal, childCount, relative age, and that the old file was kept as .bak; when the prior sidecar is missing/corrupt the helper does NOT throw and prints a generic overwrite notice that still states a .bak was kept.
AC6 — sidecar and .bak derive from the actual outPath: invoking with --out <custom> writes <custom>.meta.json and <custom>.bak and does NOT touch the default .bober/fleet-expand.json paths.

AC7+AC8 — the on-disk manifest still passes FleetManifestSchema.safeParse and carries NO provenance keys; write-and-stop default and the --yes gate are unchanged (no blocking prompt); npm run build + full npm test pass with the existing fleet suites green and their overwrite-notice assertions updated. (The 6 pre-existing tests/e2e/cockpit-integration MCP failures are unrelated and may remain.)

generatorNotes:
Read src/fleet/index.ts:196-360 — the two Step-4 write blocks (runFleetExpand :207-224 and runFleetExpandDeep :343-360) are near-identical: resolve outPath = opts.out ?? join(root,'.bober','fleet-expand.json'); ensureDir(dirname(outPath)); access-check alreadyExisted; randomBytes tmp; writeFile(tmp)+rename(tmp,outPath); if(alreadyExisted) console.log('[fleet expand|expand-deep] Overwritten existing manifest at: '+outPath). REPLACE both with a single call to a NEW helper in src/fleet/manifest-write.ts:
  writeManifestWithProvenance({ outPath, manifest, provenance: { command, goal, critique, childCount }, log?, now? }): Promise<void>
The helper must: (1) ensureDir(dirname(outPath)); (2) check if outPath exists; (3) if it exists, try to read `${outPath}.meta.json` (tolerate missing/corrupt → null), move the existing manifest to `${outPath}.bak` (rename), and log an informative notice — with prior sidecar: `[${command}] Replacing manifest from \`${prior.command}\` for goal "${prior.goal}" (${prior.childCount} children, ${relAge}) → kept as ${basename(outPath)}.bak`; without prior sidecar: a generic `[${command}] Overwriting existing manifest at ${outPath} → kept as ${basename}.bak`; (4) atomically write the new manifest (randomBytes tmp + rename, mirror index.ts:217-220); (5) write `${outPath}.meta.json` with { command, goal, critique, childCount, timestamp: new Date(now()).toISOString() }. Derive sidecarPath/bakPath from outPath (NOT the default constant). Inject the clock as `now?: () => number` defaulting to Date.now; relative-age helper formats (now - Date.parse(prior.timestamp)) as e.g. 'just now' / 'Nm ago' / 'Nh ago'. Pass console.log as the default `log`. In index.ts, call it from BOTH sites: expand → { command:'fleet expand', goal, critique:false, childCount: manifest.children.length }; expand-deep → { command:'fleet expand-deep', goal, critique: opts.critique === true, childCount: manifest.children.length }. Use the RAW `goal` param (NOT goalWithHint). Keep Step 5 (print manifest) and the --yes gate exactly as-is. Provenance NEVER goes into the manifest object. Update the overwrite-notice assertions in expand.test.ts / expand-deep.test.ts / expand-deep-critique.test.ts to the new message (search for 'Overwritten existing manifest'). Add src/fleet/manifest-write.test.ts with a temp-dir based suite (mirror how other fleet tests create temp dirs) injecting a fixed clock. Commit: 'bober(sprint-1): fleet manifest provenance sidecar + recoverable overwrite'.
evaluatorNotes: Verify independently: (1) read manifest-write.ts — confirm sidecarPath/bakPath are derived from outPath (e.g. `${outPath}.meta.json` / `${outPath}.bak`), NOT hardcoded to the default. (2) A test writes A then B to the same outPath and asserts `${outPath}.bak` === A bytes and outPath === B (recoverability) — run it. (3) A test deletes/corrupts the prior sidecar and asserts writeManifestWithProvenance does NOT throw and still prints an overwrite notice + writes a new sidecar. (4) Notice determinism: a fixed injected clock yields an exact asserted relative-age string. (5) --out test writes <custom>.meta.json/.bak and leaves the default path untouched. (6) The written manifest still passes FleetManifestSchema.safeParse and has no provenance keys (grep the written file / parse it). (7) No blocking prompt / stdin use added; write-and-stop + --yes unchanged. (8) git diff: index.ts Step-4 blocks replaced by helper calls (the two old console.log 'Overwritten existing manifest' lines gone); manifest.ts, decomposer*.ts, critic-deep.ts, runFleet untouched. (9) npm run build + full npm test green; the ONLY failures permitted are the 6 pre-existing tests/e2e/cockpit-integration MCP 'Connection closed' failures (unrelated). Required strategies: build + typecheck; lint + unit-test.
